All right, what's happening? So in this video, I'm going to show you how to figure, or rather configure, a sales conversion in your Google account. So when you sell stuff from your ads, it'll track it, in theory. Their tracking is not that great, but no tracking is really that great. But some tracking is better than none. So here we go. We're right where we were earlier. If you need to get to this dashboard, you go to Tools and Settings and we click on Conversions, and it'll take us here. We already have the opt-in conversion that we made, and we called it a sign-up. I'm going to make a new one by plus, by pressing, pressing, depending on how you spell it, the blue plus thing. We're going to do the same thing, which is the website conversion. And now this category is going to be a purchase. And we're going to call this, I don't know, bought something. We're the only ones who see this, by the way, right? So if you're selling multiple things, and you're tracking the conversions of multiple things, you've got all sorts of different things you're offering, and you're funneling, you could do it, name it, whatever. So I'm just kind of messing around. Now in this case, let's just say we're selling something for 300 bucks or whatever. We're going to say use the same value for each conversion, and the thing we're selling is $300. So I just put 300 right there, 100. And so that's how we do that. And we're going to recommend it that this thing gets counted once per time, because let's say they go to the thank you page twice to read the instructions or whatever. We don't want to count that as two conversions. It'll mess it up. Now we can mess with all of this stuff if we want to, but I'm cool with all of this as is. So we click on create and continue. So we haven't really done anything different, except for we just said, all right, this is a purchase, and it's worth 300 bucks. And so again, we're going to use Google Tag Manager, which is a lot of that cutting and pasting. So we come over here, and this is what we're going to cut and paste. But we're going to do it a little tiny bit differently, but not much differently. So we come back over here to our Google Tag Manager. Now we already published the little thing that tracks all of our opt-ins here. We don't have to go to our website. We don't have to do anything ever again. This is why Google Tag Manager is so awesome. So what we do is you just want to end up here. Sorry, this is the account I'm working on right here. So you want to get to your main page, and we just want to go back to tags. And we're going to create a new one. And we're going to call it whatever we want to call it. All right, purchased. Something. There we go. Sounds about right. That's a good grammar. Now we click on Tag Configuration, and this is going to be another Google Ads conversion tracking thing. Probably don't have to, but I'm going to. And then we're good. We're set. So that's all we had to do, except for tell it what needs to happen in order for this conversion to actually occur, which is, of course, they go to your thank you page. So we're going to have contains. And that is mech.com forward slash bought. Boom. So if that's where people go after they buy our stuff, I got to hit save. So let me do that. But if this is where people go after they buy our stuff, when they go here, after clicking on one of our ads on the YouTubes, then it'll count as a conversion. Like super simple. So I'm going to click save. And the way we set it up in AdWords is we said, hey, I only count this once. So if the guy goes twice or whatever, don't be double counting it. And it works pretty good there. So I clicked save. I'm going to click save again. A lot of clicking. We've pasted. We've clicked. And once again, you always have to hit the submit thing. It's so easy to forget to do. And you're like, why is it a damn thing? And it's because you forget to hit submit. I do it all the time. So don't forget to hit submit. What are we going to do? We're going to hit, say it with me, submit like that. And then publish. And one other thing continue. We can make a little thing. Hey, I made this little change. But I never do that. I just click on continue. So you ain't done until you see that dumb little thing that says sing like nobody was listening. That one little thing. Like that is nothing's working until you saw that little thing appear. It appeared for like a split second. And now we're good. Now we're cool. And so we are now officially, OK, I'll hit Next here. And done. We are now officially tracking conversions in Google Ads. And we're doing it through Google Tag Manager, which just makes everything way easier. Makes everything work better. And it's easy. So that's how you do it. Ain't nothing to it. If you want to track a gazillion different opt-in pages and stuff, then you just do this as many times as you need to. So there you have it.
